zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開篇論點

6月1日由能源因素引發的熊市趨平在週二部分反轉，但其核心政策訊息依然存在。2年期美國公債殖利率維持在4.05%，10年期收於4.46%，30年期則收於4.97%。這種依到期年限而異的走勢，反映長端溫和上漲，但前端仍承載聯準會升息風險。這既非重返先前的寬鬆敘事，也不是新一波期限溢價衝擊。投資人削減部分通膨補償及長天期實質殖利率壓力，同時維持存續期間部位要進一步全面上漲的高門檻。

國內證據解釋了漲勢為何未能延伸至前端。4月職缺增至762萬個，創近2年最強讀數，裁員人數則下降。因此，在這份數據讓決策官員有餘裕聚焦通膨後，美國公債自盤中最佳水準回落。克里夫蘭聯準銀行總裁 Beth Hammack強化了這種不對稱性：在不確定性下，維持利率不變仍屬合理，但若價格壓力持續，可能很快需要採取行動。因此，收盤表現結合了附息債券的溫和喘息，以及對政策敏感的2年期券種持續面臨的阻力。

全球發展形成第二重限制。歐元區整體、核心與服務通膨均加速，強化市場對ECB升息的預期。英格蘭銀行決策官員Megan Greene也主張，隨能源衝擊持續，收緊政策的理由正日益增強。日本10年期公債標售穩健且波蘭通膨走軟，顯示高殖利率正在吸引買盤，且通膨衝擊並不一致。即便如此，較廣泛的已開發市場訊號仍是前端利率居高不下，並存在對存續期間資金的競爭。

信用與跨資產表現，為防禦性解讀提供了決定性的反證。Blue Owl Technology Finance與1家和CoreWeave相關的資料中心借款人，都在改善相較初步討論的定價之際，擴大已完成的債券發行規模。股市上漲、半導體類股大漲，波動率則下降。因此，美國公債漲勢與融資市場運作順暢，以及AI相關風險偏好強勁並不衝突。固定收益市場的主要結論是受控的重新定價：油價衝擊的邊際力道有所減弱，但勞動需求具韌性、央行反應函數偏鷹，以及資本形成活動活躍，使實質利率與政策預期維持限制性。

## 美國公債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美國公債表現隨到期年限增加而改善。2年期殖利率持平於4.05%，5年期下降1個基點至4.17%，10年期下跌1個基點至4.46%，30年期則下降2個基點至4.97%。本交易日透過2年期以上券種的小幅牛市趨平，反轉週一部分由前端領跌的走勢。長存續期間部位的回升最為明顯，但變動幅度顯示這只是獲得喘息，而非市場對通膨或Fed政策路徑作出決定性的重新評估。

殖利率曲線指標顯示，剩餘壓力集中在對政策敏感的到期年限附近。2s10s斜率收窄1個基點至41個基點，2s30s則收窄2個基點至92。2s5s10s蝶式利差下降1個基點至-17，使5年期券種相對兩翼仍然偏貴；2s10s30s蝶式利差則維持-10。這種形狀符合以下市場狀態：在先前跌勢後，市場得以恢復部分中期與長期存續期間部位，但尚未消除最終升息所附帶的溢價。

抗通膨債券將溫和的名目債券漲勢拆分為不同管道。10年期實質殖利率持平於2.07%，10年期損益兩平通膨率則下降1個基點至2.39%；因此，名目10年期殖利率的變動來自通膨補償略為下降。在更長端，20年期實質殖利率下降1個基點至2.48%，30年期實質殖利率下跌2個基點至2.69%，與長端較強的表現一致。5年期實質殖利率上升1個基點至1.65%，使較接近殖利率曲線中段的實質政策壓力得以維持。

盤中走勢顯示，勞動數據的重要性高於收盤微幅變化所呈現的程度。在Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey顯示職缺自3月的689萬個增加，並高於687萬個的預估中值前，美國公債曾接近過去1個月的最佳水準。裁員減少也增添了經濟趨穩訊號。細節好壞參半，足以避免債市重新遭到拋售，但整體組合削弱短期寬鬆的理由，並使殖利率自低點回升。

Hammack的反應函數，使勞動市場韌性得以直接傳導至政策。她形容勞動市場接近充分就業，表示目前政策利率可能不具限制性，並強調商品及非住房服務皆面臨廣泛價格壓力。4月PCE通膨年增3.8%，進一步加深她的疑慮。交換合約定價反映，市場預期12月前約升息18個基點，並於2027年3月前完整升息25個基點。前端維持錨定，是因目前的耐心仍伴隨明確的升息選項。

能源使這項選擇維持相關性，但週二未再引發衝擊。WTI與Brent收高，惟仍位於週一區間內，使美國公債得以保有溫和漲幅。油價近期自5月高點回落，已使各到期年限殖利率約下降10至20個基點，因此當日走勢是能源重新定價過程中的暫停，而非其告終。收盤表現顯示，原油如今同時影響預期通膨與政策時點，而勞動市場韌性則決定Fed能以多快速度回應。

前端供給順利去化。美國財政部在42天期國庫券標售中接受約$750億投標，最高得標利率為3.625%，投標倍數為3.28，高於前次標售的2.71。間接投標人取得已接受競爭性投標的63.61%，直接投標人取得3.62%，交易商則取得32.77%。此結果確立了市場對規模較小之6週期發行的需求；其6月4日交割仍是另一項獨立現金事件。在沒有發行前交易可供比較下，投標倍數與配售情況比截標利率本身更具資訊含量。

國庫券收盤殖利率未顯示共同的融資壓力。4週期及8週期券種的票息等值殖利率各下降1個基點至3.69%及3.70%，6週期殖利率則上升1個基點至3.70%。13週期與17週期券種分別持平於3.71%及3.74%，26週期與52週期殖利率則各下降1個基點。這種狹窄分散程度，加上標售投標倍數增強，支持前端流動性有序的判斷，即使預期政策路徑依舊堅挺。

部位配置使這波漲勢容易受到正反兩向的新證據影響。交易員已削減部分最極端的看空曝險，但調查顯示，投資人已從小幅直接做多轉回中性，選擇權活動則持續同時對漲勢與跌勢避險。因此，收盤反映油價壓力緩解與經濟具韌性之間的暫時平衡。若要出現更大的存續期間走勢，仍須勞動市場與服務業證據轉弱，或能源壓力再度增強至足以改變政策路徑。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

整體信用市場同時呈現掛牌商品報酬近乎持平、短期融資秩序良好，以及已完成的初級市場交易表現強勁。LQD下跌0.01%，HYG上漲0.08%，JNK則上漲0.04%。這些變動過小，不足以顯示現券利差出現廣泛變化，但與其他市場未見風險部位遭清算的情況相符。發行人的執行結果更具資訊性：投資等級、銀行資本及高收益借款人均獲得需求，但定價能力因結構與標的資產品質而異。

Blue Owl Technology Finance Corp.提供了最清楚的投資等級債承接案例。這家私募信貸業務發展公司原先目標為$3億，最終售出$5億債券。票券定價為較美國公債高265個基點，較初步討論的約300個基點收窄，Fitch預期評等為BBB-。資金用途指定為償還現有債務。發行規模上調且利差收窄，顯示儘管該基金前一季將資產價值下調約5%，仍可進入市場融資。

這項發行結果並未消除投資組合風險。Blue Owl先前已表示，對軟體業放款正轉趨審慎；該基金則在資產價值下調後維持股利。因此，投資人是在獲得明確補償下，為最低投資等級的再融資提供資金，並非對所有私募信貸曝險一視同仁地表示認可。此結果延續前一交易日的證據：當價格、贊助機構能力及債務管理用途具可信度時，業務發展公司仍能發債。

UBS顯示資本結構更下層也有需求。該銀行以美元籌集$15億的其他第1類資本資本，債券殖利率定價為6.875%，指引則由初始約7.5%收窄至7.25%-7.375%。這些證券最早可於2032年下半年贖回。這是瑞士暫停會影響息票支付能力與替代發行的規則修訂提案後，UBS第1次發行AT1。定價改善顯示，監管不確定性下降支撐次順位債需求，但較長的贖回期仍保留重大延長及損失吸收風險。

高收益市場以不同條件承接AI基礎設施債券。Prime Data Centers的間接子公司Elk Grove Village Property將發行規模增加$5,000萬至$9億。5年期債券以面額定價，殖利率為7.5%，位於討論區間低端。這座位於芝加哥地區的設施已全數出租給CoreWeave，租期15年，代表約$22億的合約收入。該租約支撐發行結果，但興建風險、租戶集中度及投機等級交易對手，仍是信用評估的核心。

這筆交易也釐清科技相關融資的規模。資料中心業者年內已透過風險較高的債券籌集超過$270億，而科技公司更廣泛的債券發行規模則超過$3,000億。目前美國公債定價看來仍主要由Fed及能源預期驅動，但供給管道正在擴大。同日承接強勁，顯示買方能區分具合約保障的基礎設施現金流與無擔保的AI熱潮；但這並未消除企業發債與主權債存續期間之間的長期競爭。

南韓的融資替代情形顯示，在市場准入失靈前，當地殖利率上升已會改變企業行為。截至4月止年度，企業發行741.4兆韓元的短期債券與商業本票，較1年前增加47.1%，企業債發行則下降34.1%。3年期AA評等無擔保債券殖利率已上升近100個基點至4.4%，3個月期商業本票則為3.06%。借款人正縮短負債期限以控制當前成本，但若短期利率上升，將提高未來的續借敏感度。

市政預付能源融資構成另一條結構型供給管道。California Community Choice
Financing Authority核准發行最高$12.5億債券，為Pioneer Community
Energy取得折價的長期電力供應，並由Goldman Sachs擔任承銷商。在這類結構
中，銀行或保險公司收取並投資發債所得，支付能源採購所需款項，再以公用事
業繳納的能源費用償還債券；投資人通常可取得高於同評級市政債券的殖利率。
該市場今年發行量已達$146億，較2025年同期增加94%，延續自2022年以來
每年創高的趨勢。另一方面，Bank of America預計擔任Black Belt Energy
Gas District另一筆$11.5億預付天然氣債券的資金接收方。這項成長顯示公共
能源借款人能夠擴大融資來源，但也增加市政債供給，並使中介機構信用、能源
合約與付款機制成為市場承接能力的核心。

英國官方交易數據，為繁忙的美國初級市場提供較安靜的反向力量。第1季已完成的國內、流入與流出型併購案由495件降至352件，流入型交易價值則由£330億降至£142億。併購案源轉弱，可能減少英鎊信用市場短期過橋及永久性融資需求。與美國再融資及AI發債之間的對比相當重要：在資本支出或債務替換具迫切性之處，供給仍然活躍；其他市場的企業交易量較為疲弱，則減輕發債壓力。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

歐元區通膨強化了全球前端殖利率的底部。5月消費者物價漲幅由3.0%加速至3.2%，核心通膨升至2.5%，服務通膨則達3.5%。漲勢範圍廣泛，強化市場對ECB於6月11日升息的預期，即使區域商業活動正以2023年以來最快速度萎縮。對美國公債而言，其傳導管道是相對政策定價與期限溢價競爭：歐洲成長轉弱可支撐存續期間，但更廣泛的通膨回應，將縮小美國獨立採行寬鬆敘事的空間。

ECB官員將初步升息視為保險，同時讓後續路徑取決於條件。Gediminas Simkus主張及時採取行動以避免通膨加速，Olli Rehn則表示，儘管需要採取行動，通膨預期仍然穩定。這項分歧對全球殖利率曲線影響重大。若只調整1次以維護公信力，壓力將集中在歐洲前端；若有證據顯示服務業成本持續轉嫁，則升息循環可能延長，並提高全球投資人對較長天期主權債券所要求的報酬，包括美國公債。

英國也呈現相同的政策與成長衝突。Megan Greene表示，隨伊朗衝突延長能源成本轉嫁，近期可能需要升息；貨幣市場已完全定價9月前升息25個基點，並認為年底前再升息的機率為85%。總裁 Andrew Bailey則主張，由於金融狀況已收緊且需求脆弱，決策官員還有一些時間。這項歧見使英鎊前端波動率維持高檔，同時限制市場對疲弱成長本身即可拉低長期殖利率的信心。

然而，英國公債的高絕對殖利率仍吸引策略性買方。管理$8,360億資產的Insight Investment，隨遠期殖利率達到約6%或以上，持續增持較長天期英國債券。30年期英國公債殖利率超過5.5%，在21個已開發市場中最高。當估值足以補償通膨與財政風險時，這類需求可為存續期間部位提供緩衝。這也說明債券漲勢為何能與偏鷹的央行論辯並存：買方可能在票息收益足夠高時接受風險，但不代表其認定緊縮政策已經結束。

日本同日出現更強的存續期間漲勢。10年期JGB殖利率下降11個基點至2.57%，此前標售投標倍數達3.53，高於過去12個月平均的3.35。殖利率觸及1996年以來最高水準後，市場認為估值便宜，加上長端情緒改善，吸引買盤。然而，隔夜交換仍顯示BOJ本月採取行動的機率約為76%，日圓則逼近160兌1美元。因此，標售帶來的喘息緩和了眼前全球供給壓力，但未解決政策或跨境買方基礎風險。

中國處於相反的景氣位置。中國人民銀行僅進行人民幣2億元的7天期附買回操作，創史上最低單日規模，試圖收回因放款成長疲弱而產生的過剩流動性。中國的10年期政府公債殖利率週一收於1.70%，創8月以來最低，隨後長債期貨走低。中國人民銀行正抑制殖利率過快下降，但其低水準仍為其他地區受通膨驅動的緊縮趨勢，提供全球成長面的反向力量。

波蘭顯示，即使在歐洲內部，輸入性通膨壓力也不一致。5月通膨由3.2%放緩至3.1%，低於3.6%的預估中值後，該國央行連續第3個月將政策利率維持在3.75%。官員仍將能源、寬鬆財政政策、薪資成長及燃料管制列為風險。這項決定支持有選擇性的存續期間解讀：實際通膨轉弱可在當地延後升息，但歐元區與英國的證據仍使較廣泛的已開發市場政策衝擊偏鷹。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

新興市場債券表現優於平靜的美國信用指標，EMB上漲0.26%，美元指數則大致持平於99.22。儘管油價上升，這項組合仍使整體硬通貨環境維持穩定。各國情況則更加分散。智利在風險指標改善之際尋求額外市場融資；塞內加爾更深陷困境；1家巴西企業借款人則在債券強勁上漲後探索債務重整。美元流動性仍然可得，但政治公信力與負債結構決定誰能受益。

塞內加爾是最明顯的主權債例外。該國2031年到期美元債券交易價格接近面額的54 美分，低於2月中旬68 美分的高點；其主權利差則比美國公債高逾1,500個基點。該國3檔美元債券年內下跌2.5%，相較之下，整體新興市場美元債券指數上漲1.5%。總裁 Bassirou Diomaye Faye與總理 Ousmane Sonko之間的內閣衝突，使財政問題更加惡化，也使此案不再只是一般的高貝塔跌勢。

償債計算使這場政治爭端具有實質影響。IMF在發現隱匿負債後，已暫停1項$18億援助計畫。投資人預期塞內加爾將支付本月到期的$9,250萬息票款項，但政府同時面臨CFA 2,960億、約$5.24億的區域市場償付款項。即使近期息票獲得支付，這項組合仍提高市場對更廣泛債務重整的預期。這是主權償債能力及援助計畫風險，有別於整體新興市場的溫和正報酬。

智利從較穩健的起點面臨供給問題。總裁 Jose Antonio Kast的政府要求Congress額外授權$62億債務，疊加於已獲准的$174億之上；該國年內已籌集超過$80億。政府已將赤字預估由前任政府的GDP 1.8%上調至2.4%，並可能發行多達$60億外幣債務。額外外債將增加硬通貨再融資管道，即使其也擴大了融資選項。

市場並未將智利的請求視為立即陷入困境：主權債殖利率小幅下降，違約保護成本則跌至2月中旬以來最低。此一反應顯示，投資人在考量赤字擴大之餘，也重視該國持續擁有市場准入及擬議的支出約束。其與塞內加爾之間的分歧相當重要。若制度與市場准入仍具可信度，規模較大的借款授權可獲市場承接；隱匿債務及多邊融資中斷，則可能使規模較小的短期付款時程演變為償債能力事件。

Braskem呈現新興市場內另一種企業重整管道。這家巴西石化公司在7月付款前，尋求債權人支持庭外程序。若持有其1/3債務的債權人提供支持，可能促成90天暫緩期，之後則需取得多數同意才能敲定最終方案。消息公布後，其股價曾下跌6.3%；即使其債券過去3個月報酬約為44%，遠高於新興市場企業債指標的0.6%。這項反轉反映事件與資本結構風險，而非巴西主權風險。

## 跨資產

原油仍是美國公債漲勢最直接的複雜因素。WTI上漲1.74%至$93.76，Brent上漲1.07%至$96.00，但兩者收盤均位於週一區間內。其1個月跌幅分別約為8.0%及11.3%，顯示邊際通膨衝擊已自高峰緩解，即使價格仍處高檔。這種組合與10年期損益兩平通膨率下降1個基點相符：能源並未迫使市場展開新一波通膨重新定價，但仍高得不足以排除Fed的升息選項。

股市否定了殖利率下降的防禦性解讀。S&P 500上漲0.13%，Nasdaq 100上漲0.48%，半導體指數則大漲5.87%。AI相關樂觀情緒可與長天期美國公債殖利率溫和下降並存，因為當日債市走勢反映的是油價與政策校準，而非對需求崩潰的恐懼。半導體優異表現也保留了資本支出與未來債券供給管道，即使當前科技業發債並非收盤走勢的主導因素，長期而言仍對存續期間構成挑戰。

波動率進一步證實風險偏好。VIX下降1.74%至15.77，高收益總報酬基金小幅上漲，已完成的新券發行定價也較初步討論改善。投資人願意承接股票與信用風險，同時容許長端上漲。這種組合類似通膨壓力受到控制地下降，並無廣泛去槓桿或資金轉進政府債券的跡象。

美元與黃金仍未化解地緣政治問題。美元指數僅上漲0.02%，限制了外部借款人的額外緊縮衝擊；黃金則上漲0.31%至$4,489.10。美元定價穩定支撐新興市場債券的正報酬，但黃金展現韌性且原油維持高檔，使市場仍有因應衝突與通膨的避險需求。因此，跨資產一方面證實融資與風險環境良性，另一方面也對能源與政策環境已恢復正常的說法提出質疑。

## 市場前瞻意義

美國勞動市場與服務業數據將從6月3日08:15 ET公布的ADP民間就業開始，接著是10:00 ET的ISM服務業指數及14:00 ET的褐皮書；初領失業救濟金人數於6月4日公布，就業報告則於6月5日08:30 ET發布。若ADP接近120,000人的市場共識、服務業接近53.8且就業與價格堅挺，非農就業成長則約達或高於85,000人的市場共識，失業率維持4.3%，將證實JOLTS反映持久的勞動市場趨穩。2年期與5年期殖利率、實質利率定價及12月升息溢價對此最為敏感。若招聘普遍轉弱、服務價格走軟且初領失業救濟金人數上升，將挑戰Hammack以通膨為優先的解讀，並重新打開週二尾盤反轉所壓抑的寬鬆尾端情境。

6月3-4日的美國財政部操作將提供下一項承接檢驗。$690億的17週期國庫券標售於6月3日11:30 ET截止，接著於13:40至14:00 ET進行上限為$20億的長存續期間流動性支援買回。6月4日，$750億的4週期及8週期國庫券標售後，將進行最高$125億、涵蓋1個月至2年期附息券的現金管理買回。投標倍數強勁、非交易商需求廣泛、國庫券殖利率走勢有序且交割順暢，將確認週二42天期標售所傳達的平穩融資訊號，並支撐企業持續發債。若投標疲弱、短期殖利率上升或現金壓力明顯，則將挑戰初級市場可持續承接公私部門供給且無須擴大讓利的觀點。

石油流向與下一波央行訊號，將決定週二的全球通膨壓力是否同步。BOJ總裁 Kazuo Ueda於6月3日發表談話，印度央行於6月5日決定政策，ECB則於6月11日開會。若原油持續下跌、能源進口國貨幣轉強，且政策指引將成本轉嫁視為受控，將挑戰目前偏鷹的政策同步，並降低損益兩平通膨率、海外市場前端殖利率及長天期美國公債所隱含的期限溢價。若供應再度中斷、亞洲貨幣轉弱、印度央行的回應聚焦通膨，或ECB措辭暗示不只升息1次，將確認衝擊正在擴大。即使美國成長仍具韌性，這種結果仍會重啟週一的熊市趨平風險，並加劇石油進口國主權債的再融資壓力。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為準。

overall_summary.headline: 油價壓力緩解帶動美國公債溫和牛市趨平，但勞動市場使前端保持堅挺
overall_summary.session_narrative: 截至已完成的紐約收盤，美國公債漲幅隨到期年限增加，殖利率曲線溫和趨平；在指標10年期券種，通膨補償下降的貢獻大於實質殖利率提供的支撐。職缺增強，加上Hammack表示若物價壓力持續則願意升息，使債券自盤中最佳水準回落。融資秩序良好、新券成功發行且風險資產堅挺，削弱了防禦性轉進存續期間部位的理由。
overall_summary.summary: 較長天期美國公債收復部分週一由能源因素引發的跌幅，對政策敏感的前端則維持堅挺。通膨補償走軟支撐存續期間，但職缺展現韌性、全球央行反應函數偏鷹，以及融資市場活躍，均阻止漲勢進一步擴大。

section_summaries.market_read: 這波漲勢反映邊際能源壓力消退，而非限制性政策退場。
section_summaries.next_check: 勞動市場與服務通膨必須轉弱，這波喘息才可能延伸至前端。
section_summaries.summary: 較長天期美國公債收復部分先前由能源因素引發的跌幅，但勞動需求維持韌性，使前端保有升息溢價。通膨補償緩解、全球政策訊號偏鷹，以及初級市場運作順暢，使收盤表現屬於受控的重新定價，而非寬鬆循環重啟。

section_summaries.market_read: 殖利率曲線獲得的支撐仍附帶條件，因為政策風險集中於短期與中期券種。
section_summaries.next_check: 非農就業、服務價格與初領失業救濟金人數將決定前端升息溢價是否持續。
section_summaries.section_title: 美國公債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 2年期以上券種小幅上漲，帶動殖利率曲線牛市趨平，2年期殖利率則持平。名目債券漲勢主要源自損益兩平通膨率下降；職缺堅挺，加上Fed明確保留升息選項，維持了實質政策壓力，也使存續期間部位持續暴露於後續數據風險。

section_summaries.market_read: 初級市場需求具選擇性，但強度足以抵銷廣泛的防禦性信用訊號。
section_summaries.next_check: 交易讓利與短期再融資選擇將顯示供給是否仍能輕易獲得承接。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 信用ETF的整體風險走勢不大，但已完成的交易顯示，投資等級債、銀行資本工具與高收益債均獲穩健承接。發行規模上調且定價收緊的交易，反映市場獎勵可信的再融資用途或合約保障的現金流；結構較弱的交易則仍面臨延長、集中度與續借風險。

section_summaries.market_read: 海外存續期間需求緩和了供給壓力，但未打破全球偏鷹的政策傾向。
section_summaries.next_check: ECB指引與亞洲貨幣反應將顯示能源成本轉嫁是否正在擴大。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 歐元區通膨升溫及英國偏鷹論辯，強化全球前端殖利率的底部，即使疲弱成長使政策更加複雜。日本及英國長天期債券在高殖利率下需求強勁，為存續期間部位提供緩衝；中國與波蘭則顯示各地通膨及流動性狀況仍不均衡。

section_summaries.market_read: 整體美元流動性仍然充裕，但公信力與債務結構主導個別結果。
section_summaries.next_check: 塞內加爾的息票與區域市場償付款項將檢驗，即時付款能否避免債務重整。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 美元環境穩定且整體新興市場債券走強，掩蓋了發行人層面的劇烈分化。智利儘管新增借款需求，仍保有市場准入；塞內加爾的隱匿債務及援助計畫危機則加深重整風險；Braskem顯示企業負債事件可能與主權環境脫鉤。

section_summaries.market_read: 風險資產證實這是有序重新定價，而非逃離成長或槓桿。
section_summaries.next_check: 油價能否維持方向，以及亞洲貨幣走勢，將檢驗通膨壓力是否再度加速。
section_summaries.summary: 原油上漲，但仍處於前一交易日區間內且低於5月高點，使損益兩平通膨率得以下降，卻未消除通膨風險。股市上漲、半導體強勁且波動率下降，顯示美國公債走強反映政策校準，而非成長恐慌；黃金則保有地緣政治避險功能。

section_summaries.market_read: 唯有成長證據、融資操作與能源訊號維持良性，收盤走勢才可能延續。
section_summaries.next_check: 關注6月3-5日美國數據、6月3-4日美國財政部操作及6月11日ECB決策。
section_summaries.summary: 短期論點取決於3項檢驗：美國勞動市場與服務業是否證實需求具韌性、美國財政部操作能否維持融資秩序，以及油價與央行訊號是否使全球通膨壓力同步。每項檢驗都會直接影響前端殖利率、供給承接能力或熊市趨平風險是否重現。

watch_events.market_implication: 若獲確認，將支撐2年期與5年期殖利率、實質利率及12月升息溢價。
watch_events.market_trigger: 非農就業成長接近或高於85,000人、失業率為4.3%，且服務價格堅挺，將確認經濟趨穩。
watch_events.title: 美國勞動市場與服務業韌性檢驗
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤非農就業、初領失業救濟金人數、服務業就業與價格，並與經濟趨穩論點相互比對。

watch_events.market_implication: 順利執行將支撐供給承接，並使企業得以在無須擴大讓利下持續發債。
watch_events.market_trigger: 投標倍數強勁、非交易商廣泛參與且短期殖利率走勢有序，將確認融資環境平穩。
watch_events.title: 美國財政部標售與買回承接
watch_events.watch_line: 關注標售投標倍數、非交易商需求、國庫券殖利率、交割及買回流動性。

watch_events.market_implication: 更廣泛的壓力將推升損益兩平通膨率與海外市場前端殖利率，重燃美國公債熊市趨平風險。
watch_events.market_trigger: 石油供應再度中斷、亞洲貨幣轉弱，或指引暗示ECB不只升息1次，將確認壓力正在擴大。
watch_events.title: 油價與央行政策同步
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤原油、能源進口國貨幣，以及BOJ、印度央行與ECB對成本轉嫁的指引。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The June 1 energy-driven bear flattening partially unwound on Tuesday, yet its central policy message survived. The 2-year Treasury yield held at 4.05%, the 10-year finished at 4.46%, and the 30-year ended at 4.97%. That maturity pattern was a restrained long-end rally around a front end still carrying Federal Reserve tightening risk. It was neither a return to the earlier easing narrative nor a fresh term-premium shock. Investors reduced some inflation compensation and long real-yield pressure while preserving a high hurdle for a broader duration advance.

Domestic evidence explains why the rally stopped short of the front end. April job openings rose to 7.62 million, the strongest reading in almost 2 years, and layoffs declined. Treasuries consequently retreated from their best levels as the release gave policymakers room to concentrate on inflation. Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack reinforced that asymmetry: holding rates steady remained reasonable amid uncertainty, though persistent price pressure could soon require action. The close therefore combined modest relief in coupons with continued resistance at the policy-sensitive 2-year point.

Global developments added a second constraint. Euro-area inflation accelerated across headline, core and services measures, strengthening expectations for an ECB increase. Bank of England policymaker Megan Greene also argued that the case for tighter policy was growing as the energy shock persisted. A solid Japanese 10-year auction and softer Polish inflation showed that high yields were attracting buyers and that the inflation impulse was not uniform. Even so, the broader developed-market signal remained one of elevated front-end rates and competition for duration capital.

Credit and cross-asset behavior supplied the decisive counterweight to a defensive interpretation. Blue Owl Technology Finance and a CoreWeave-linked data-center borrower both increased completed bond offerings while improving pricing from initial discussions. Equities advanced, semiconductor shares surged, and volatility fell. The Treasury rally was therefore compatible with functioning financing markets and strong AI-linked risk appetite. The main fixed-income conclusion is a controlled repricing: the oil shock lost some marginal force, but resilient labor demand, hawkish central-bank reaction functions and active capital formation kept real rates and policy expectations restrictive.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury performance improved with maturity. The 2-year yield was unchanged at 4.05%, the 5-year declined 1 basis point to 4.17%, the 10-year fell 1 basis point to 4.46%, and the 30-year dropped 2 basis points to 4.97%. The session reversed part of Monday’s front-end-led selloff through a small bull flattening beyond 2 years. Long duration recovered most clearly, though the size of the move showed relief rather than a decisive reassessment of inflation or the Fed path.

Curve measures located the remaining pressure near policy-sensitive maturities. The 2s10s slope narrowed 1 basis point to 41 basis points, and 2s30s narrowed 2 to 92. The 2s5s10s butterfly moved 1 basis point lower to -17, leaving the 5-year sector relatively rich against its wings, while the 2s10s30s butterfly held at -10. This shape fits a market that could restore some intermediate and long duration after the prior selloff without removing the premium attached to an eventual rate increase.

Inflation-linked securities separated the modest nominal rally into different channels. The 10-year real yield was unchanged at 2.07%, while the 10-year breakeven declined 1 basis point to 2.39%; the nominal 10-year move therefore came through slightly lower inflation compensation. Farther out, the 20-year real yield fell 1 basis point to 2.48% and the 30-year real yield dropped 2 to 2.69%, matching the stronger long-end performance. The 5-year real yield rose 1 basis point to 1.65%, preserving real-policy pressure closer to the belly.

The intraday path made the labor release more important than the small closing changes suggest. Treasuries had been near their best levels of the past month before the Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey showed available positions rising from 6.89 million in March and exceeding the 6.87 million median estimate. Fewer layoffs added to the stabilization signal. Details were mixed enough to avoid a renewed selloff, yet the combination weakened the case for near-term easing and pulled yields back from their lows.

Hammack’s reaction function gave that labor resilience a direct policy transmission. She described the labor market as near full employment, said the current policy rate may not be restrictive, and emphasized broad price pressure across goods and non-housing services. April PCE inflation at 3.8% year on year reinforced her concern. Swap contracts priced about 18 basis points of tightening by December and a full quarter-point increase by March 2027. The front end remained anchored because current patience still carried an explicit tightening option.

Energy kept that option relevant without producing another shock on Tuesday. WTI and Brent settled higher but stayed within Monday’s ranges, allowing Treasuries to retain modest gains. Recent declines from May oil peaks had already pulled yields across maturities down by roughly 10 to 20 basis points, so the day’s move was a pause in the energy repricing rather than its resolution. The close suggests that crude now influences both expected inflation and the timing of policy, with labor resilience determining how readily the Fed can respond.

Front-end supply cleared cleanly. Treasury accepted about $75.0 billion in the 42-day bill auction at a 3.625% high rate and a 3.28 bid-to-cover ratio, up from 2.71 at the previous sale. Indirect bidders took 63.61% of accepted competitive tenders, direct bidders 3.62%, and dealers 32.77%. The result established demand for the smaller 6-week offering; its June 4 settlement remained a separate cash event. With no when-issued comparison, the cover and allocation carry more information than the stop alone.

Bill closing yields showed no common funding strain. 4- and 8-week coupon-equivalent yields each fell 1 basis point to 3.69% and 3.70%, while the 6-week yield rose 1 to 3.70%. The 13- and 17-week tenors were unchanged at 3.71% and 3.74%, and the 26- and 52-week yields each declined 1 basis point. This narrow dispersion, together with the auction’s stronger cover, supports an orderly front-end liquidity picture even as the expected policy path stayed firm.

Positioning left the rally vulnerable to incoming evidence in both directions. Traders had pared some of their most extreme bearish exposure, yet surveys showed investors moving from a small outright long back to neutral and options activity continued to hedge both rallies and selloffs. The close consequently reflected a temporary balance between oil relief and a resilient economy. A larger duration move still requires either weaker labor and service-sector evidence or renewed energy pressure strong enough to change the policy path.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

The broad credit picture combined nearly flat listed returns, orderly short-term funding and strong completed primary deals. LQD slipped 0.01%, while HYG rose 0.08% and JNK gained 0.04%. Those moves were too small to signal a broad change in cash spreads, but they fit the absence of risk liquidation seen elsewhere. Issuer execution was more informative: investment-grade, bank-capital and high-yield borrowers all found demand, with pricing power varying by structure and underlying asset quality.

Blue Owl Technology Finance Corp. provided the clearest investment-grade absorption case. The private-credit business-development company sold $500 million of bonds after initially targeting $300 million. The notes priced at 265 basis points over Treasuries, tightening from initial discussion around 300 basis points, and carried an expected BBB- rating from Fitch. Proceeds were designated to repay existing debt. The upsize and tighter spread showed access despite the fund having reduced the value of its assets by about 5% in the prior quarter.

That execution did not erase portfolio risk. Blue Owl had already said it was becoming more cautious toward software lending, while the fund maintained its dividend after the asset-value reduction. Investors were therefore funding a refinancing at the bottom of investment grade with visible compensation, not endorsing every private-credit exposure equally. The result extended the previous session’s evidence that business-development companies can issue when price, sponsor capability and debt-management purpose are credible.

UBS demonstrated demand farther down the capital structure. The bank raised $1.5 billion of Additional Tier 1 capital in dollars, pricing the notes at a 6.875% yield after guidance tightened from roughly 7.5% initially to 7.25%-7.375%. The securities are first callable in late 2032. This was UBS’s first AT1 sale since Switzerland paused proposed rule changes affecting coupon capacity and replacement. The pricing improvement showed that reduced regulatory uncertainty supported subordinated demand, while the long call preserved meaningful extension and loss-absorption risk.

High yield absorbed AI infrastructure on distinct terms. Elk Grove Village Property, an indirect Prime Data Centers subsidiary, increased its offering by $50 million to $900 million. The 5-year bonds priced at par to yield 7.5%, the low end of discussion. The Chicago-area facility is fully leased to CoreWeave for 15 years and represents about $2.2 billion of contracted revenue. That lease supported execution, though construction, tenant concentration and the speculative-grade counterparty remain central to the credit case.

The deal also clarified the scale of technology-related funding. Data-center businesses had raised more than $27 billion from riskier bonds during the year, even as technology companies had sold more than $300 billion of bonds more broadly. Current Treasury pricing still appeared driven chiefly by Fed and energy expectations, but the supply channel is building. Strong same-day absorption shows that buyers can distinguish contracted infrastructure cash flow from unsecured AI enthusiasm; it does not eliminate the longer-run competition between corporate issuance and sovereign duration.

Funding substitution in South Korea showed how higher local yields alter corporate behavior before access fails. Companies issued 741.4 trillion won of short-term bonds and commercial paper in the year through April, up 47.1% from a year earlier, while corporate-bond issuance fell 34.1%. The yield on 3-year AA-rated nonguaranteed bonds had risen almost 100 basis points to 4.4%, whereas 3-month commercial paper stood at 3.06%. Borrowers were shortening liabilities to contain current cost, increasing future rollover sensitivity if short rates rise.

Municipal prepaid-energy finance added a separate structured-supply channel.
The California Community Choice Financing Authority approved as much as $1.25
billion of bonds to acquire discounted long-term electricity for Pioneer
Community Energy, with Goldman Sachs named as underwriter. In these structures,
a bank or insurer receives and invests the proceeds, makes the payments needed
to procure energy, and relies on the utility's energy payments to repay
bondholders, who generally receive more yield than on similarly rated municipal
debt. Issuance in the segment had reached $14.6 billion for the year, up 94%
from the same period of 2025 and extending annual records set since 2022.
Separately, Bank of America was slated to act as funding recipient for Black
Belt Energy Gas District's $1.15 billion prepaid-gas bond. The growth shows that
public-power borrowers can widen their financing base, but it also enlarges
municipal supply and makes intermediary credit, energy contracts, and payment
mechanics central to absorption.

Official UK transaction figures supplied a quieter counterforce to the busy US primary market. Completed domestic, inward and outward acquisitions fell to 352 in Q1 from 495, and inward deal value declined to £14.2 billion from £33.0 billion. A weaker acquisition pipeline can reduce near-term bridge and permanent financing demand in sterling credit. The contrast with US refinancing and AI issuance matters: supply remained active where capital spending or debt replacement was urgent, while softer corporate transaction volume reduced issuance pressure elsewhere.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

Euro-area inflation reinforced the global floor under front-end yields. May consumer prices accelerated to 3.2% from 3.0%, core inflation quickened to 2.5%, and services reached 3.5%. The breadth of the increase strengthened expectations for an ECB rate rise on June 11, even as regional business activity contracted at its fastest pace since 2023. For Treasuries, the transmission comes through relative policy pricing and term-premium competition: weaker European growth can support duration, but a broader inflation response reduces the scope for an independent US easing narrative.

ECB officials treated the initial increase as insurance while leaving the later path conditional. Gediminas Simkus argued for timely action to prevent acceleration, and Olli Rehn said expectations remained anchored even though action was needed. The split is consequential for global curves. 1 adjustment aimed at preserving credibility would concentrate pressure at the European front end; evidence of sustained services pass-through could extend the cycle and raise the return global investors demand from longer sovereign debt, including Treasuries.

Britain displayed the same policy-growth conflict. Megan Greene said a near-term increase could be needed as the Iran conflict prolonged energy pass-through, and money markets fully priced a quarter-point rise by September with an 85% chance of another by year-end. Governor Andrew Bailey argued that policymakers had some time because financial conditions had already tightened and demand was fragile. The disagreement keeps sterling front-end volatility elevated while limiting confidence that weak growth alone will pull long yields lower.

High absolute gilt yields nevertheless attracted strategic buyers. Insight Investment, which manages $836 billion, had been adding longer-dated UK bonds as forward yields reached around or above 6%. 30-year gilts yielded more than 5.5%, the highest among a group of 21 developed markets. That demand can cushion duration when valuations compensate for inflation and fiscal risk. It also shows why a rally can coexist with a hawkish central-bank debate: buyers may accept the risk at sufficiently high carry without concluding that policy tightening is finished.

Japan provided a stronger same-day duration rally. The 10-year JGB yield fell 11 basis points to 2.57% after an auction produced a 3.53 bid-to-cover ratio, above its 3.35 12-month average. Perceived cheapness and improved long-end sentiment drew buyers after yields had reached their highest since 1996. Yet overnight swaps still assigned about a 76% chance to a BOJ move during the month, and the yen was approaching 160 per dollar. Auction relief therefore eased immediate global supply pressure without resolving the policy or cross-border buyer-base risk.

China occupied the opposite cyclical position. The People’s Bank of China conducted only 200 million yuan of 7-day reverse repos, the smallest daily operation on record, as it sought to absorb excess liquidity that had accompanied weak loan growth. China’s 10-year government yield had ended Monday at 1.70%, its lowest since August, before long-bond futures slipped. The PBOC was leaning against an overly rapid yield decline, yet the low level still supplied a global-growth counterweight to inflation-driven tightening elsewhere.

Poland showed that imported inflation pressure was not uniform even within Europe. The central bank held its key rate at 3.75% for a 3rd month after May inflation slowed to 3.1% from 3.2%, below the 3.6% median forecast. Officials still cited energy, loose fiscal policy, wage growth and fuel regulation as risks. The decision supports a selective duration interpretation: softer realized inflation can delay tightening locally, while the euro-area and UK evidence keeps the wider developed-market policy impulse hawkish.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market debt outperformed the calm US credit gauges, with EMB rising 0.26%, while the dollar index was essentially unchanged at 99.22. That combination kept the broad hard-currency backdrop stable despite higher oil. Country evidence was far more dispersed. Chile sought additional market funding while its risk measures improved, Senegal moved deeper into distress, and a Brazilian corporate borrower explored restructuring after a powerful bond rally. Dollar liquidity was available, but political credibility and liability structure determined who benefited.

Senegal was the clearest sovereign exception. Its 2031 dollar bond traded near 54 cents, down from a mid-February peak of 68 cents, and the sovereign spread exceeded 1,500 basis points over Treasuries. The country’s 3 dollar notes had lost 2.5% during the year against a 1.5% gain for a broader emerging-market dollar-bond index. A cabinet conflict between President Bassirou Diomaye Faye and Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko compounded the fiscal problem and pushed the case beyond a routine high-beta selloff.

Debt-service arithmetic made the political dispute consequential. The IMF had suspended a $1.8 billion program after hidden obligations were uncovered. Investors expected Senegal to make $92.5 million of coupon payments due during the month, yet the government also faced CFA 296 billion, or about $524 million, of regional-market repayments. The combination increased expectations of a broader restructuring even if the immediate coupon is paid. This is sovereign solvency and program risk, distinct from the modestly positive broad EM return.

Chile faced a supply problem from a stronger starting point. President Jose Antonio Kast’s government asked Congress to authorize another $6.2 billion of debt on top of $17.4 billion already permitted, after raising more than $8 billion during the year. The administration had increased its deficit forecast to 2.4% of GDP from the previous government’s 1.8% estimate and could issue as much as $6 billion in foreign currency. Additional external debt would increase the hard-currency refinancing channel even as it broadens funding options.

Markets did not treat the Chilean request as immediate distress: sovereign yields edged lower and the cost of default protection fell to its lowest since mid-February. That reaction suggests investors gave weight to the country’s continuing access and proposed expenditure restraint alongside the wider deficit. The divergence from Senegal is important. A larger borrowing authorization can be absorbed when institutions and market access remain credible; concealed debt and disrupted multilateral financing can turn a smaller near-term payment schedule into a solvency event.

Braskem illustrated a separate corporate restructuring channel inside emerging markets. The Brazilian petrochemicals company sought creditor backing for an out-of-court process before July payments. Support from holders of 1/3 of its debt could enable a 90-day standstill, followed by the majority approval needed for a final plan. Its shares fell as much as 6.3% on the news even though its bonds had returned about 44% over 3 months, far above a 0.6% EM corporate gauge. The reversal exposed event and capital-structure risk, not sovereign Brazil risk.

## Cross asset

Crude remained the most direct complication for the Treasury rally. WTI rose 1.74% to $93.76 and Brent gained 1.07% to $96.00, but both settled inside Monday’s ranges. Their 1-month declines of about 8.0% and 11.3% showed that the marginal inflation shock had eased from its peak even as prices stayed high. That mix fits the 1-basis-point decline in 10-year breakevens: energy did not force a new inflation repricing, yet it remained too elevated to remove the Fed’s tightening option.

Equities rejected a defensive reading of lower yields. The S&P 500 gained 0.13%, the Nasdaq 100 rose 0.48%, and the semiconductor index surged 5.87%. AI-linked optimism could coexist with modestly lower long Treasury yields because the day’s bond move reflected oil and policy calibration, not fear of collapsing demand. The semiconductor outperformance also preserved the capital-spending and future debt-supply channel, adding a longer-run challenge to duration even as current technology issuance was not the dominant driver of the close.

Volatility reinforced that risk appetite. The VIX declined 1.74% to 15.77, while high-yield total-return funds posted small gains and completed new issues improved from initial price discussions. Investors were willing to absorb equity and credit risk while allowing the long end to rally. That configuration resembles a controlled reduction in inflation pressure, with no sign of broad deleveraging or a flight into government bonds.

The dollar and gold left the geopolitical question unresolved. The dollar index added only 0.02%, limiting an additional tightening impulse for external borrowers, while gold rose 0.31% to $4,489.10. Stable dollar pricing supported the positive EM debt return, but gold’s resilience and elevated crude preserved demand for protection against conflict and inflation. Cross assets therefore confirmed the benign funding and risk backdrop while challenging any claim that the energy-policy regime had normalized.

## Forward Market Significance

The US labor and services sequence begins with ADP private payrolls at 08:15 ET on June 3, followed by ISM services at 10:00 ET and the Beige Book at 14:00 ET; claims arrive June 4 and the employment report at 08:30 ET on June 5. ADP near the 120,000 consensus, services near 53.8 with firm employment and prices, and payroll growth around or above the 85,000 consensus with unemployment holding at 4.3% would confirm that JOLTS reflects durable labor stabilization. The 2- and 5-year yields, real-rate pricing and the December tightening premium would remain most sensitive. Broadly weaker hiring, softer service prices and rising claims would challenge Hammack’s inflation-first interpretation and reopen the easing tail that Tuesday’s late-session reversal suppressed.

Treasury operations over June 3-4 provide the next absorption test. The $69.0 billion 17-week bill auction closes at 11:30 ET on June 3, followed by a long-duration liquidity-support buyback capped at $2.0 billion from 13:40 to 14:00 ET. On June 4, the $75.0 billion 4- and 8-week auctions precede a cash-management buyback of up to $12.5 billion in 1-month to 2-year coupons. Strong cover, broad nondealer demand, orderly bill yields and smooth settlement would confirm the calm funding signal from Tuesday’s 42-day sale and support continued corporate issuance. Weak bidding, higher short yields or visible cash pressure would challenge the view that primary markets can keep absorbing public and private supply without a larger concession.

Oil flows and the next central-bank signals determine whether Tuesday’s global inflation pressure becomes synchronized. BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda speaks on June 3, the RBI decides policy on June 5, and the ECB meets on June 11. A sustained crude decline, firmer energy-importer currencies and guidance that treats pass-through as contained would challenge the current hawkish alignment, easing breakevens, overseas front ends and the term premium embedded in long Treasuries. Renewed supply disruption, weaker Asian currencies, an RBI response focused on inflation, or ECB language pointing beyond 1 increase would confirm that the shock is broadening. That outcome would reopen Monday’s bear-flattening risk and intensify refinancing pressure for oil-importing sovereigns even if US growth remains resilient.

section_summaries.market_read: The rally reflected fading marginal energy pressure, not a retreat from restrictive policy.
section_summaries.next_check: Labor and service inflation must soften before relief can extend through the front end.
section_summaries.summary: Longer Treasuries recouped part of the prior energy-led selloff, but the front end retained a tightening premium as labor demand stayed resilient. Easing inflation compensation, hawkish global policy signals and functioning primary markets left the close as controlled repricing rather than a renewed easing cycle.

section_summaries.market_read: Curve relief remains conditional because policy risk is concentrated in short and intermediate maturities.
section_summaries.next_check: Payrolls, service prices and claims will determine whether the front-end tightening premium persists.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Small gains beyond 2 years produced a bull flattening while the 2-year yield held steady. The nominal rally came mainly through lower breakevens, as firm job openings and an explicit Fed tightening option preserved real-policy pressure and left duration exposed to incoming evidence.

section_summaries.market_read: Primary-market demand was selective but strong enough to counter a broad defensive credit signal.
section_summaries.next_check: Deal concessions and short-term refinancing choices will show whether supply remains easily absorbed.
section_summaries.summary: Credit ETFs showed little broad risk movement, but completed deals revealed solid absorption across investment grade, bank capital and high yield. Upsized offerings and tighter pricing rewarded credible refinancing or contracted cash flows, while weaker structures retained extension, concentration and rollover risks.

section_summaries.market_read: Overseas duration demand softened supply pressure without breaking the global hawkish policy bias.
section_summaries.next_check: ECB guidance and Asian currency reactions will show whether energy pass-through is broadening.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Hotter euro-area inflation and hawkish UK debate reinforced a global floor under front-end yields, even as weak growth complicated policy. Strong demand at Japanese and long-dated UK yields cushioned duration, while China and Poland showed that inflation and liquidity conditions remained uneven.

section_summaries.market_read: Broad dollar liquidity remained available, but credibility and debt structure drove outcomes.
section_summaries.next_check: Senegal’s coupon and regional repayments will test whether immediate payment prevents restructuring.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: Stable dollar conditions and firmer broad EM debt masked sharp issuer-level divergence. Chile retained market access despite added borrowing needs, while Senegal’s hidden-debt and program crisis deepened restructuring risk; Braskem showed that corporate liability events can detach from sovereign conditions.

section_summaries.market_read: Risk assets confirmed an orderly repricing rather than a flight from growth or leverage.
section_summaries.next_check: Sustained oil direction and Asian currency moves will test whether inflation pressure re-accelerates.
section_summaries.summary: Crude rose but stayed within the prior session’s range and below May peaks, allowing breakevens to ease without removing inflation risk. Advancing equities, strong semiconductors and lower volatility showed that Treasury gains reflected policy calibration rather than growth fear, while gold preserved a geopolitical hedge.

section_summaries.market_read: The close can extend only if growth evidence, funding operations and energy signals stay benign.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch the June 3-5 US data, June 3-4 Treasury operations and June 11 ECB decision.
section_summaries.summary: The near-term thesis hinges on 3 tests: whether US labor and services confirm resilient demand, whether Treasury operations preserve orderly funding, and whether oil and central-bank signals synchronize global inflation pressure. Each test directly affects front-end yields, supply absorption or renewed bear-flattening risk.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

UST 2s10s curve 41 bp (-1 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 92 bp (-2 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Treasuries bull-flattened modestly as oil pressure eased, but labor kept the front end firm
overall_summary.session_narrative: At the completed NY close, Treasury gains increased with maturity and the curve flattened modestly, with lower inflation compensation doing more work than real-yield relief at the benchmark 10-year point. Stronger job openings and Hammack’s willingness to tighten if prices persist pulled bonds from their best levels. Orderly funding, successful new issues and firm risk assets limited the case for a defensive flight into duration.
overall_summary.summary: Longer Treasuries recovered part of Monday’s energy-driven selloff, while the policy-sensitive front end held firm. Softer inflation compensation supported duration, but resilient job openings, hawkish global reaction functions and active financing markets prevented a broader rally.

watch_events.market_implication: Confirmation would sustain 2- and 5-year yields, real rates and the December tightening premium.
watch_events.market_trigger: Payroll growth near or above 85,000, 4.3% unemployment and firm service prices would confirm stabilization.
watch_events.title: US labor and services resilience test

watch_events.market_implication: Clean execution would support supply absorption and continued corporate issuance without a larger concession.

watch_events.market_implication: Broader pressure would lift breakevens and foreign front ends, reviving Treasury bear-flattening risk.

watch_events.title: Oil and central-bank synchronization
